message:
fix(ars): iterative lineage traversal and UTF-8 safe string truncation

Two targeted bug fixes in the ARS subsystem:

ars_evolve.rs — is_ancestor() now uses iterative lineage traversal
instead of collecting the entire ancestry chain via lineage(). The
previous implementation allocated a Vec of all ancestors then searched
it with .contains(), which is O(n) space and O(n) time. The new version
walks the lineage HashMap parent-by-parent, returning true as soon as
the ancestor is found. This avoids the allocation entirely and exits
early on match.

ars_explain.rs — render_card() and pad_right() now use .chars().count()
instead of .len() for string width calculations, and .chars().take(n)
instead of byte-offset slicing for truncation. The previous code used
byte lengths which would panic on multi-byte UTF-8 characters (e.g.,
CJK text, emoji) when a slice boundary fell mid-character. The fix
ensures safe truncation regardless of character encoding.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
